title: cerasus × nudiflora
intro: Cerasus × nudiflora, also known as the flowering cherry or naked cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is native to East Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cerasus × nudiflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and bark can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
